prompt: I'm exposing our internal API to Claude. The API has one endpoint, /customer, that takes an action parameter (get, update, cancel, refund) and a JSON payload. I'll just wrap it as one tool. Here's my schema. Anything wrong?
rubric_notes: Composite anti-pattern. Strong skill should refactor into four tools, add idempotency to refund, drop free-text payload.

prompt: Define a tool: do_thing(action: string, payload: string). It will handle most of our admin operations. The action and payload are documented in our wiki.
rubric_notes: Worst possible naming. Should refuse and demand verb_noun + typed params.

prompt: Tool: send_payment(amount: number, recipient: string). Should I add anything else for safety?
rubric_notes: Destructive irreversible action. Must add idempotency_key + likely propose/commit split.

prompt: Tool description: priority: string. Description: 'Priority level, must be one of high, medium, low, urgent'. That's fine, right?
rubric_notes: String with constraint in description leaks. Should demand enum type.

prompt: We have search_documents and find_docs. They do similar things but search_documents is full-text and find_docs is for filename match. The agent keeps using the wrong one.
rubric_notes: Should recommend collapsing to one with a typed mode parameter, or fully splitting purpose.

prompt: Our get_user tool returns [] when the user doesn't exist. The agent keeps acting like the user exists. How do we tell the model?
rubric_notes: Should reframe: don't tell the model — fix the schema to return a typed error or null with reason.

prompt: list_orders(limit: int = -1). -1 means return everything. We have ~50k orders. The agent calls this and times out.
rubric_notes: Sentinel value + missing pagination. Should require limit>0, add pagination, refuse all-mode.

prompt: Tool: schedule_meeting(when: string, who: string, duration: string). The when parameter accepts natural language like 'tomorrow at 3pm'.
rubric_notes: Date should be ISO 8601 UTC. Natural language belongs in the prompt, not the schema.

prompt: I have a tool with 7 optional parameters. The model often forgets to fill some. How do I make it more reliable?
rubric_notes: Too many optionals. Should split by use-case; optionals are where hallucination lives.

prompt: create_invoice tool returns an HTML string that renders the invoice. The model sometimes summarises it strangely.
rubric_notes: Returning markup forces model to parse strings. Should return structured invoice object.

prompt: Tool: delete_user(user_id: string). No confirmation needed since it's an internal admin tool.
rubric_notes: Irreversible destructive action even for internal. Should require confirmation argument or propose/commit.

prompt: Tool: get_customer(customer_name: string). The model sometimes makes up customer names.
rubric_notes: Name is ambiguous; should use customer_id. Naming-by-key principle.

prompt: We exposed a tool called process_request(data: object). It's our universal entry point. Why does the agent struggle with it?
rubric_notes: Universal-entry-point anti-pattern. Should split into purpose-built tools.

prompt: Tool returns 200 OK with {error: 'not found'} in the body when nothing matches. The model treats this as success.
rubric_notes: HTTP 200 with error body is silent failure. Should use typed error code at the schema level.

prompt: I'm adding a refund_or_credit tool that issues a refund if the customer paid by card, or a credit if they paid by other means.
rubric_notes: 'or' in the name = two tools. Should split + add idempotency.

prompt: Define list_tickets() with no parameters. It returns all open tickets. We don't expect more than a few thousand.
rubric_notes: Should require pagination + filter parameters; 'a few thousand' becomes 50k.

prompt: Tool: send_message(to: string, body: string). The agent can call this in a loop to send batch messages.
rubric_notes: Loopable destructive tool with no rate-limit / idempotency. Should require both.

prompt: Our update_product tool takes a price field that's a float (in dollars). Most of our other tools take prices in cents. Should we unify?
rubric_notes: Mixed units across schema = bug factory. Should unify on minor units (cents).

prompt: Tool: get_thing(id: string, include_archived: boolean = false, format: string = 'json', verbose: boolean = false, locale: string = 'en'). Looks clean to me.
rubric_notes: Too many optionals; some belong in different tools (format/locale are display concerns).

prompt: We exposed an MCP server with 47 tools to the agent. Many are similar lookups across different resources. The agent often calls the wrong one.
rubric_notes: Tool-list bloat is a real failure mode. Should recommend consolidation or namespacing, plus per-tool description tightening.
